metadata.notes:
agent_supports: yes (update_plan tool)
daemon_capability: bug — codex parser routes update_plan through the generic function_call path; the plan[] step/status array never reaches SessionMetrics.Tasks, so the session API tasks field stays empty

assessment.body:
## Verdict

agent_supports: yes
daemon_capability: bug; driver_capability: ready

## Reasoning

### agent_supports: yes

Codex's task-list equivalent is the **`update_plan`** tool. Claude Code uses `TaskCreate`/`TaskUpdate` and OpenCode uses `todowrite` (full-list replace); codex ships `update_plan`, a full-list-replace TODO/checklist tool. The codex binary itself is the authoritative source: its embedded tool catalog declares `update_plan` — "Updates the task plan." — with a `plan` array of steps, each step carrying a `status` that is "One of: pending, in_progress, completed". The base instructions state codex's capabilities include "making & updating plans", and a dedicated `## update_plan` prompt section instructs the model: "To create a new plan, call `update_plan` with a short list of 1-7-word steps with a `status` for each step (`pending`, `in_progress`, or `completed`) ... mark each finished step as `completed` and the next step you are working on as `in_progress`. There should always be exactly one `in_progress` step until everything is done." A real `update_plan` function_call in a committed codex transcript carries exactly the scenario's structure: `{"plan":[{"step":"...","status":"in_progress"},{"step":"...","status":"pending"}, ...]}`. The tool runs inside the agent's own session — it renders a checklist to the user, spawns no child session, and creates no ParentSessionID link, so SubagentCount stays 0. This is the same single-session task-list primitive the scenario asserts, distinct from foreground/background-subagent (which use a real spawn).

### daemon_capability: bug

A local trace EXISTS but the codex parser mis-handles it, so the load-bearing observable — the three items surfacing in the session API `tasks` field with subject + status — is NOT produced today.

- The codex transcript records each `update_plan` call as a `function_call` event whose `arguments` JSON holds the full `plan[]` step/status array (verified in a committed recording). The signal reaches the Source the daemon tails — this is NOT a cloud-only / unobservable behavior.
- The codex parser (`parser.go`) dispatches on event TYPE, not tool NAME: `update_plan` falls into the generic `function_call` / `response_item(type=function_call)` branch and becomes an opaque `ToolUse{ID:call_id, Name:"update_plan"}`. The parser emits NO `TaskDelta` and NO `TaskSnapshot` for it (it special-cases only `task_complete`/`turn_aborted`→`turn_done` and `<proposed_plan>`→`ExitPlanMode`).
- The replay/daemon `tasks` summary (`core/application/replayengine/metrics.go` copies `m.Tasks`; the tailer fills `m.Tasks` only from parser-emitted `TaskDelta`/`TaskSnapshot`; `session.SessionMetrics.Tasks` is documented "populated from TaskCreate / TaskUpdate ... Nil for ... non-Claude-Code adapters"). With no codex task path, the codex session's `tasks` field stays empty.

Classification: `bug`, not `incapable` — a trace is present and structurally complete; the daemon simply lacks a codex `update_plan`→Task mapping (the analog of opencode's `todowrite` synthesis). It is the product/parser's job to add it; file an issue. The OTHER user-observable signals hold: `update_plan` auto-executes (no permission gate), so the arc is a clean ready → working → ready and SubagentCount stays 0 — those phases pass; only the tasks-content assertion is `known_failing`.

### driver_capability: ready

The scenario is elicited with `send` / `wait_turn` / `sleep` steps, all present in codex's `driver-interactive.sh` (confirmed via recipe-lint: send, wait_turn, sleep, slash, plus session/fork/interrupt/restart/sigkill). No missing step type, so `ready`.

## Provisional emission caveat

The `agent=yes` verdict is grounded in the binary's tool catalog + prompt AND a real committed `update_plan` call, so emission is confirmed, not merely inferred from the parser. The `daemon=bug` verdict is likewise grounded in a present trace, not a guess. Confidence 0.8: the gap mechanism is certain from code, but live recording will confirm that a 3-item walk to all-completed emits the expected function_call sequence and that the daemon indeed leaves tasks empty (promoting the bug from doc/code-derived to recording-proven).
caveats: update_plan is full-list-replace (like opencode todowrite), not incremental TaskCreate/TaskUpdate deltas — a future codex Task path would need to reconcile each snapshot the way opencode's metrics.go does, not apply per-item deltas.

The codex binary notes 'update_plan is a TODO/checklist tool and is not allowed in Plan mode' — recording must drive codex in normal (non-Plan) mode so the tool is available; the recipe does not enter Plan mode.

The tasks-content assertion is not expressible in the phase DSL (mirroring claudecode/opencode task-list, whose tasks pin lives in the replay golden's summary.tasks). For this known_failing cell the phase spec asserts the lifecycle arc + SubagentCount=0 (which pass) and the meta flags known_failing for the tasks-content observable.

Codex emits an intermediate assistant message before tool calls; the parser's task_complete→turn_done handling prevents working→ready flicker, so the tool_loop_working invariant holds — but a live recording should confirm no spurious ready dip mid-plan.

sources.note: Dispatches on event TYPE not tool NAME: update_plan → generic function_call → opaque ToolUse{Name:"update_plan"}. Emits no TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot (only task_complete/turn_aborted→turn_done, <proposed_plan>→ExitPlanMode).
Lines 40-51: the session/golden tasks field is copied verbatim from m.Tasks (the tailer's accumulated task list); empty for codex because the parser emits no task deltas.

SessionMetrics.Tasks doc: 'populated from TaskCreate / TaskUpdate tool calls in the Claude Code transcript. Nil for sessions that have not used TaskCreate (including non-Claude-Code adapters).' Task struct = {ID, Subject, Status: pending|in_progress|completed}.
TaskDelta / TaskSnapshotEntry are defined as Claude Code TaskCreate/TaskUpdate constructs; the tailer folds them into its tasks slice. opencode synthesises them from todowrite; codex emits neither.

Committed codex transcript with 17 real update_plan function_calls; arguments = {"plan":[{"step":"...","status":"in_progress"},{"step":"...","status":"pending"},...]} — proves the local trace exists and carries subject(step)+status per item.

Strings scan of the codex native binary (@openai/codex@0.135.0): update_plan tool 'Updates the task plan.' with steps status 'One of: pending, in_progress, completed'; base_instructions 'making & updating plans'; '## update_plan' prompt section detailing the pending→in_progress→completed walk; 'update_plan is a TODO/checklist tool and is not allowed in Plan mode'.

preconditions: codex CLI on PATH and authenticated (~/.codex/config.toml or OPENAI_API_KEY); min version 0.50.0
irrlichd is recording — either run-cell.sh in isolated mode, or --attach against a running daemon started with --record
codex is NOT in Plan mode (update_plan is unavailable in Plan mode) — the interactive driver launches the normal REPL
setup: The interactive driver launches `codex --no-alt-screen` in a fresh per-run cwd under <staging>/cwd/, accepts the trust dialog, and waits for the TUI banner + MCP boot before sending the prompt
No auto-mode step is needed: update_plan auto-executes with no permission gate (capabilities permission_hooks:false), so the session never enters waiting
Each send bumps the expected-turn counter; wait_turn blocks until a new event_msg/task_complete line appears in the rollout, so the trailing sleep never races a still-running turn

script.text: Use your update_plan tool to track exactly three short steps, in this order: 'read README', 'summarize README', 'reply done'. Walk them one at a time: call update_plan to mark each step in_progress and then completed before moving to the next, keeping exactly one in_progress until all are done. Do NOT spawn any subagent — keep everything in this one session. When all three steps are completed, reply with exactly the word: done

verify: events.jsonl shows the rollout session born ready, then a single ready->working transition when the prompt is sent
transcript.jsonl contains at least three function_call blocks with name=update_plan, the final one carrying all three steps at status=completed (the plan[] step/status array)
transcript.jsonl contains NO function_call with name resembling a subagent spawn; no parent_linked events and no child rollout sessions appear (SubagentCount stays 0)
events.jsonl contains NO state_transition to waiting (update_plan auto-executes, no permission gate)
events.jsonl shows a working->ready transition at turn end; final session state is ready

KNOWN FAILING: the session API tasks field stays EMPTY (the codex parser emits no TaskDelta/TaskSnapshot for update_plan) — the correct behavior is three items with matching subjects and final status; this gap is the filed bug
